authors: Al-Kaisy A, Van Buyten JP, Carganillo R, Caraway D, Gliner B, Subbaroyan J, Panwar C, Rotte A, Amirdelfan K, Kapural L
title: 10 kHz SCS therapy for chronic pain, effects on opioid usage: Post hoc analysis of data from two prospective studies
study_question: Does 10 kHz SCS reduce opioid use and pain in chronic back and leg pain patients?
population_assessed: Adults with chronic low back and/or leg pain receiving 10 kHz SCS, mainly failed back surgery syndrome.
outcome_measures: Opioid dose MME/day, back pain VAS, leg pain VAS
inclusion_criteria: Adults with chronic back and/or leg pain, VAS at least 5 cm, SCS candidates.
answer_to_study_question: 10 kHz SCS reduced opioid dose and high-risk opioid use while significantly improving back and leg pain.
reasoning: Most fields showed unanimous agreement. For study design and sample size, Anthropic and OpenRouter aligned; Google differed despite lower design confidence and likely counted completers.
field_level_decisions_json: {"study_design": "Chose prospective_cohort_controlled from two models; article is a post hoc analysis of prospective studies, not a standalone trial.", "number_of_participants_in_study": "Used 137 from two models; 129 likely reflects a subset or follow-up sample.", "volume_issue_pages": "Preferred concise consensus format 9:11441 over expanded pagination.", "population_assessed": "Merged shared description with failed back surgery syndrome detail from higher-confidence Anthropic output."}
